meta.lastUpdated: After the PGA Championship · 2 of 4 majors complete

prizes.label: Overall Pool Champion
prizes.desc: Most cumulative earnings, all four majors

prizes.label: Group Champion
prizes.desc: Most cumulative earnings within your group of 8

participants.name: Arif Nuraney
participants.blurb: Two majors, two different winners on your card — Rory at Augusta, Aaron Rai's PGA miracle at Aronimink. You're not leading the Coleclough Cup, you're lapping it. Infuriatingly, actual skill may be involved.

participants.name: Matt Cavelti
participants.blurb: Riding Aaron Rai's PGA lottery ticket and Scheffler's quiet brilliance to second overall. Statistically the most dangerous man not named Arif — and you know it.

participants.name: Kevin Young
participants.blurb: Banked a monster Masters then watched the PGA happen to other people. Still third, but the lead is evaporating one quiet weekend at a time.

participants.name: Ali Pejman
participants.blurb: Front-loaded everything at Augusta and has been coasting on fumes ever since. Cam Smith and Wyndham Clark need a reminder that they play golf for a living.

participants.name: Adam Hill
participants.blurb: Augusta hero, Aronimink ghost. Your entire PGA roster collectively earned what Rory tips his caddie. Top five for now — enjoy it.

participants.name: Michael O'Callaghan
participants.blurb: Rode Rory and a tidy Masters into the lead pack, then the PGA reminded you Tyrrell Hatton exists. Clinging to the top six by your fingernails.

participants.name: Jeff Williams
participants.blurb: Seventh overall and STILL not the best in your own group — Cavelti owns Group 2 and holds the deed to your house. Max Homa, my guy, throw him a bone.

participants.name: Jamie MacDougall
participants.blurb: Top eight overall, almost entirely because Rory carried a team that includes JJ Spaun — the reigning US Open champ — missing two cuts. Bold roster construction.

participants.name: Chris Cooper
participants.blurb: You've got Scheffler AND Rahm and somehow only ninth — but nobody in the entire pool has a better shot at the US Open. It's Shinnecock on Sunday or bust, pal.

participants.name: Phil Kuzyk
participants.blurb: Scheffler keeps you respectable while Robert MacIntyre and Max Homa take turns missing cuts. Top ten in spite of yourself.

participants.name: Tom Edstrand
participants.blurb: Solid, balanced, forgettable — the beige minivan of the Coleclough Cup. JT's PGA T4 was the only moment your pulse left resting heart rate.

participants.name: Rob Pettit
participants.blurb: No superstar, just six guys quietly cashing cheques. Respectable in the way a scrambling 4-over that somehow makes the cut is respectable.

participants.name: Jimmy Richmond
participants.blurb: Your PGA was electric — hello, Rahm — and your Masters was a four-hour nap. Pick a major and commit, man.

participants.name: Sean Monahan
participants.blurb: Scheffler and JT do all the heavy lifting; the back half of your roster is in witness protection. Jake Knapp DNP'ing the PGA was a quiet betrayal.

participants.name: Chris Kleaman
participants.blurb: Three Masters top-threes and you STILL slid to 15th — your roster peaked in April and immediately filed for retirement.

participants.name: Phil Kinninmont
participants.blurb: Dead weight at Augusta, then Rahm and a swarm of Ben Griffins dragged you up the PGA board. The comeback kid absolutely nobody asked for.

participants.name: Mike Richmond
participants.blurb: Xander Schauffele is the sole reason anyone remembers you're in this pool. Maddeningly consistent and utterly anonymous.

participants.name: Gareth McDonnell
participants.blurb: Xander and Justin Rose do all the work while the rest of your card auditions for 'longshot of the week.' Aldrich Potgieter is not the answer.

participants.name: Jon Greyell
participants.blurb: Scheffler's Masters basically IS your season. The PGA? Your other five forgot it was even on television.

participants.name: Jay Barre
participants.blurb: Mid-pack and trending precisely nowhere. Drafting Ryan Gerard for a T70 is the kind of decision that gets brought up at divorce proceedings.

participants.name: Sean Fairburn
participants.blurb: A roster that looks great on paper and plays like a beer-league scramble. Russell Henley missing the PGA cut felt like a personal attack, didn't it?

participants.name: Rod Armstrong
participants.blurb: Your Masters was a no-show and Rahm single-handedly rescued the PGA. Living and dying by one Spaniard's mood is no way to go through a season.

participants.name: Nils Edstrand
participants.blurb: You drafted Bryson DeChambeau for the majors and he's missed both cuts. That's not bad luck, that's a hex — Scheffler is the only thing keeping you off a milk carton.

participants.name: Hans Edstrand
participants.blurb: Decent PGA, sleepy Masters, wedged in the Group 2 traffic jam behind two other guys. An Edstrand sandwich of pure mediocrity.

participants.name: David Rose
participants.blurb: Rahm's PGA bender is carrying you while the rest of the team contributes like an unpaid intern. Ryo Hisatsune DNP'ing the Masters — inspired stuff.

participants.name: Dino Konasewich
participants.blurb: Loaded up on Masters top-threes, then the PGA mugged you in the parking lot. JJ Spaun, reigning US Open champ, missing cuts on your dime is just cruel.

participants.name: Kit Matkaluk
participants.blurb: Perfectly average in every measurable dimension. Marco Penge has earned you exactly one missed cut and a resigned shrug.

participants.name: David Puddicombe
participants.blurb: Rahm good, Bryson gone, the other four a rounding error. You're the PGA's problem child who skipped April entirely.

participants.name: Craig Langdon
participants.blurb: Ludvig's PGA T4 is your lone highlight reel. The remaining five are strictly practice-round material.

participants.name: Stephen Roberts
participants.blurb: Remarkably consistent at being firmly mid. Cam Smith's PGA T7 was the only plot twist in an otherwise flat novel.

participants.name: Nigel Coleclough
participants.blurb: It is literally YOUR cup and you're sitting 31st. JJ Spaun and Jake Knapp combined for one giant DNP-shaped crater. Embarrassing — eponymously so.

participants.name: Hugh Cooper Jr.
participants.blurb: Xander keeps the lights on; Akshay Bhatia's double missed cut keeps you humble. Mid-pack and sinking very politely.

participants.name: Dionne Delesalle
participants.blurb: The front nine of your season looked promising; the back nine you're hitting it sideways into the pines. Three guys MC'd the PGA — a true group effort in disappointment.

participants.name: Ted Reid
participants.blurb: Tommy Fleetwood and Robert MacIntyre have given you nothing but Augusta heartbreak. JT is the only one who reliably shows up sober.

participants.name: Rob Snowden
participants.blurb: You had the best Masters of anyone down here, then your ENTIRE roster vanished at the PGA — $238K total, a historic faceplant. From contender to cautionary tale in four weeks flat.

participants.name: Mark Kaufmann
participants.blurb: Xander Schauffele and five men you'd struggle to pick out of a police lineup. Tyrrell Hatton's PGA no-show did you zero favours.

participants.name: Mark Trotzuk
participants.blurb: Justin Rose is essentially your entire portfolio. The rest is a JJ-Spaun-shaped disappointment with a light Kitayama garnish.

participants.name: Jamie Finkbeiner
participants.blurb: Xander and Cameron Young flatter a roster that's otherwise allergic to weekends. Akshay Bhatia owes you a handwritten apology.

participants.name: Bryson Milley
participants.blurb: Two missed-cut machines in Fleetwood and Homa, salvaged slightly by Cam Smith. You are the human embodiment of 'it is what it is.'

participants.name: JP Wright
participants.blurb: You bet big on Bryson and Brooks — the full LIV power package — and collected two missed Masters cuts for your trouble. Loud, proud, and 40th.

participants.name: Stu Louie
participants.blurb: Brooks Koepka and Akshay Bhatia have combined to do approximately nothing. Xander is the only adult in the room and he's exhausted.

participants.name: Aaron Watson
participants.blurb: Your Masters was an actual no-show — $790K, woof — and Rahm's PGA is the sole reason you're not dead last. Nico Echavarria: who, exactly?

participants.name: Darren Moscovitch
participants.blurb: A roster full of 'pretty good' that stubbornly adds up to 'not good enough.' Sam Burns and Ludvig keep you off the floor, but barely.

participants.name: Chris Morrison
participants.blurb: Tommy Fleetwood: still majorless, still on your team, still missing cuts. The whole card reads like a list of guys who 'should' be better by now.

participants.name: James Patillo
participants.blurb: Wyndham Clark won a US Open once and now exists mainly to disappoint you. Bottom third and fading into the gallery.

participants.name: Chris MacDougall
participants.blurb: Decent Augusta, then the single worst PGA in the entire pool — $176K. Your whole team apparently booked early flights home. Yikes doesn't cover it.

participants.name: Kevin Spooner
participants.blurb: Bryson DeChambeau missing the Masters cut set the tone, and the tone is 'beige.' Quietly, anonymously 47th.

participants.name: Geoff Stollery
participants.blurb: You posted the LOWEST Masters total in the entire Cup and have been clawing out of that hole ever since. Akshay Bhatia, sadly, is not a ladder.

participants.name: Chris Gibbins
participants.blurb: Bryson again — the man is a roster-killer. Your PGA haul came in below a weekend muni skins game. Forty-ninth and praying.

participants.name: Fraser Reid
participants.blurb: Anchoring a major pool with Tommy Fleetwood is a documented cry for help. Six guys, one collective pulse, 50th place.

participants.name: Chris Ellison
participants.blurb: A roster that peaked the very day you drafted it. Joaquin Niemann and Stephan Jaeger: names, not earners.

participants.name: Tim Kehler
participants.blurb: JJ Spaun headlining your team has aged like warm milk. You're 52nd and your group's basement is starting to feel cozy.

participants.name: Theo Ong
participants.blurb: You drafted Rickie Fowler in 2026 out of either loyalty or spite, and the scoreboard shows it. Second-to-last and still sinking.

participants.name: Russell Ball
participants.blurb: Two Hojgaards and a Bryson missed cut — you built a roster entirely out of vibes and surnames. 54th feels generous, honestly.

participants.name: Tom Puddicombe
participants.blurb: Tommy Fleetwood and Robert MacIntyre: the official 'so close yet so broke' starter pack. 55th and openly praying for The Open.

participants.name: Chris Smiley
participants.blurb: Dead last in the entire Coleclough Cup. Your team is a LIV-and-longshot graveyard — Bryson, Akshay and Brooks have offered a collective shrug. The only direction left is up, if only mathematically.
